title: Unit 2 – Check-in & Station 1
description: Welcome to Wales – Bauernhof, Bergbau, Stadt & Land

vocab.ex: To help the environment, I try to buy organic products whenever I can.
Be careful with the plural form: sheep.
Is that a wool jumper? It looks nice and warm.
Beca, do city people have a real idea about what farming is like?
Whenever I think of spring, I think of cute little lambs in the countryside.
Can I have an orange? Sorry, there aren't any left.
Tidying up and cleaning the kitchen are some of my chores at home.
I don't know if I could work in a mine, it's so dark down there. Darkness scares me.
Grandma, what were your smartphones like when you were young? We didn't have smartphones back then.
Wales has a long mining history.
We're going to go hiking for one day, and your rucksack is so full!

vocab.en: Are you out of your mind?

vocab.ex: Excuse me, who does this bag belong to? I think it belongs to the lady over there.
I know she needs a new rucksack for her trip to Wales. What else does she need?
Let's book a B&B, not a hotel; it's cheaper.
Rishi's parents have got good jobs, they earn lots of money.
She took two pieces of cake for herself before anyone could get a piece. That's pretty cheeky.
Don't go into that field in your shoes, it's too wet. Where are your wellies?

vocab.en: to have a go at sth

vocab.ex: This challenge is too difficult! Well, an easy challenge wouldn't be a challenge.
Sorry, but what you're saying about Cardiff isn't completely true.
He loves chocolate, especially chocolate ice cream.
People who work in clothing shops almost always get discounts on the shop's clothes.
Yes, cities are noisy, but they're also exciting!
There's a lot of dirt on farms, so it isn't the right place for expensive clothes.

vocab.de: Wenn du nichts dagegen hast ...; Wenn es dir nichts ausmacht ...
vocab.ex: If you don't mind, I'd like to be alone for a little while, thanks.
There are over 60 national parks in the USA.
Mum, Dad: I'm too old to share a bedroom. Can we turn the attic into a room for me?
Leave those garden shoes in the shed, there's so much dirt on them!
I saw how people make tartan scarves in a wool workshop in Edinburgh.
Can I offer you something to drink? No, thanks. I'm fine.
We must decide where to go on our class trip. What options do we have?
Don't throw away that old stuff in the attic! But it's just rubbish! No, your rubbish could be somebody else's treasure.
I like those old Celtic legends with dragons and things like that.
Can we trade places? The person in front of me is tall, but I'm short. You're much taller.
